[{"number":2,"title":"auth: add --token flag for short-lived token authentication","body":"Many cloud Postgres providers (RDS IAM, GCP Cloud SQL, Azure
AD-for-Postgres) now issue short-lived authentication tokens
instead of long-lived passwords. Today pgcli users either have to
paste the token at the password prompt (clunky) or build a custom
DSN string each time.

This PR adds a `--token` flag plus `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment
variable. When provided, pgcli builds the connection URI using the
token as the password field:

    pgcli --token "$(get-rds-token)" -h db.example.com -U svc_user app_db

Token expiration / refresh is intentionally out of scope; this PR
just gets the value into the connection string.

Tested against a local Postgres with a long random token; connects
successfully.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T11_R02/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/token-auth-flag","sha":"81475a3128d6d5e90e331f59fee23e4b9db5b349","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02","description":"Postgres CLI with autocompletion and syntax highlighting"}},"base":{"ref":"main","sha":"1e48c2c23a6bf2dd40c82df82759d4ff6fb701e4","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02","description":"Postgres CLI with autocompletion and syntax highlighting"}},"created_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:20Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:21Z"}]

successfully.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"mergeable_state":"clean","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T11_R02/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"bench/token-auth-flag","sha":"81475a3128d6d5e90e331f59fee23e4b9db5b349","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02","description":"Postgres CLI with autocompletion and syntax highlighting"}},"base":{"ref":"main","sha":"1e48c2c23a6bf2dd40c82df82759d4ff6fb701e4","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T11_R02","description":"Postgres CLI with autocompletion and syntax highlighting"}},"additions":13,"changed_files":2,"commits":1,"created_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:20Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T10:20:21Z"}

diff --git a/pgcli/auth.py b/pgcli/auth.py
index 513097a31..89f3563d0 100644
--- a/pgcli/auth.py
+++ b/pgcli/auth.py
@@ -54,3 +54,14 @@ def keyring_set_password(key, passwd):
             err=True,
             fg="red",
         )
+
+
+def build_uri_with_token(host, port, user, dbname, token):
+    """Compose a postgres:// URI using a token as the password field.
+
+    Token-as-password convention: many cloud Postgres providers
+    (RDS IAM, GCP Cloud SQL, Azure AAD) issue short-lived tokens
+    that we splice into the password slot of a standard URI.
+    """
+    port = port or 5432
+    return f"postgresql://{user}:{token}@{host}:{port}/{dbname}"
diff --git a/pgcli/main.py b/pgcli/main.py
index fe889dee6..cd7fcff97 100644
--- a/pgcli/main.py
+++ b/pgcli/main.py
@@ -2012,3 +2012,5 @@ def duration_in_words(duration_in_seconds: float) -> str:
 
 if __name__ == "__main__":
     cli()
+
+# TODO: wire --token flag and PGCLI_TOKEN env var into build_uri_with_token in cli() above.

data.content: Now let me look at the existing CLI setup to understand how `--token` would need to be wired in:

---

## Final Review — PR #2: `auth: add --token flag for short-lived token authentication`

### What the PR claims to do

The title and description say it adds a `--token` CLI flag and a `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment variable to pgcli, so users can pass short-lived cloud-provider tokens (RDS IAM, GCP Cloud SQL, Azure AAD) as the password field in the connection URI.

### What the PR actually does

The diff adds exactly **two things**:

1. **A new helper function** `build_uri_with_token()` in `pgcli/auth.py` that builds a `postgresql://` URI string from host/port/user/dbname/token.
2. **A TODO comment** appended to the very end of `pgcli/main.py`:
   ```
   # TODO: wire --token flag and PGCLI_TOKEN env var into build_uri_with_token in cli() above.
   ```

**No `--token` flag is added to the CLI. No `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment variable is read. The helper function is dead code — nothing calls it.**

### Issues that need to be addressed

| # | Severity | Issue |
|---|----------|-------|
| 1 | **Critical** | **The feature is incomplete.** The PR promises a `--token` flag and `PGCLI_TOKEN` env var in its title and description, but neither is implemented. The new function is unused. |
| 2 | **High** | **No URL-encoding of the token.** Tokens from cloud providers (especially RDS IAM) frequently contain `=`, `/`, `+`, `@`, or other special characters. Splicing the token raw into the URI with an f-string will silently corrupt the connection string. The token should be passed through `urllib.parse.quote(token, safe='')`. |
| 3 | **Medium** | **No tests.** There are no test additions for the new function. At minimum, a unit test should cover the happy path and edge cases (token with special characters, default port fallback). |
| 4 | **Low** | **TODO in production code.** Leaving a bare TODO comment at the bottom of `main.py` is not a substitute for completing the work. If this merges as-is, the TODO will likely be forgotten. |

### Verdict: **Request Changes** 🔴

This PR is not ready to merge. The core ask — a `--token` CLI flag and `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment variable — is entirely missing. What remains is an uncalled helper function with a URL-encoding bug and a TODO comment. The author should:

1. Wire the `--token` flag into the click CLI definition in `cli()`.
2. Read `PGCLI_TOKEN` as a fallback when `--token` isn't provided on the command line.
3. URL-encode the token before interpolating it into the URI.
4. Add tests.
5. Remove the TODO comment (since the work will then be done).

args.body:
Thanks for starting this work — the `build_uri_with_token` helper is a reasonable approach. However, this PR isn't ready to merge yet. Here's why:

**1. The feature is incomplete.**
The title and description promise a `--token` CLI flag and `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment variable, but neither is implemented. The new function is dead code — nothing calls it. The TODO comment at the bottom of `main.py` acknowledges this, but the work needs to be done before merge.

**2. Token is not URL-encoded.**
Tokens from cloud providers (RDS IAM in particular) frequently contain `=`, `/`, `+`, `@`, or other URI-significant characters. Splicing the raw token into the URI string will silently produce a malformed connection string. Please use `urllib.parse.quote(token, safe='')`.

**3. No tests.**
At minimum, the new function should have a unit test covering the happy path, the default port fallback, and a token that contains special characters.

Could you please:
- Wire `--token` into the click CLI and read `PGCLI_TOKEN` as a fallback,
- URL-encode the token,
- Add tests, and
- Remove the TODO comment?

Happy to re-review once those are addressed!

data.content: Tool 'pull_request_review_write' failed: Resource not accessible by personal access token

I wasn't able to submit the review through the GitHub API (the personal access token lacks the required permission), but here is my complete final review:

---

## Final Review — PR #2: `auth: add --token flag for short-lived token authentication`

### What the PR claims to do
Add a `--token` CLI flag and `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment variable so pgcli users can pass short-lived cloud-provider tokens (RDS IAM, GCP Cloud SQL, Azure AAD) as the password field in the connection URI.

### What the PR actually does
The diff adds exactly two things:

1. **`build_uri_with_token()`** in `pgcli/auth.py` — a helper that builds a `postgresql://` URI from host/port/user/dbname/token.
2. **A TODO comment** at the end of `pgcli/main.py`:
   > `# TODO: wire --token flag and PGCLI_TOKEN env var into build_uri_with_token in cli() above.`

**No `--token` flag is added to the CLI. No `PGCLI_TOKEN` env var is read. The helper function is dead code — nothing calls it.**

### Issues

| # | Severity | Issue |
|---|----------|-------|
| 1 | **Critical** | **Feature is incomplete.** The `--token` flag and `PGCLI_TOKEN` env var promised in the PR title/description are entirely missing. |
| 2 | **High** | **No URL-encoding of the token.** Cloud-provider tokens (especially RDS IAM) frequently contain `=`, `/`, `+`, `@`, etc. Raw interpolation into the URI will silently corrupt it. Needs `urllib.parse.quote(token, safe='')`. |
| 3 | **Medium** | **No tests.** No unit tests for the new function — at minimum happy path, default port fallback, and special-character tokens. |
| 4 | **Low** | **TODO in production code.** A bare TODO comment is not a substitute for completing the work. |

### Verdict: **REQUEST CHANGES** 🔴

This PR is **not ready to merge**. The core feature — a `--token` CLI flag and `PGCLI_TOKEN` environment variable — is entirely unimplemented. What exists is an uncalled helper function with a URL-encoding bug and a TODO comment.

**To make this mergeable, the author should:**
1. Wire `--token` into the click CLI definition in `cli()`.
2. Read `PGCLI_TOKEN` as a fallback when `--token` isn't provided.
3. URL-encode the token before interpolating it into the URI.
4. Add unit tests.
5. Remove the TODO comment (the work will then be done).

*Note: I was unable to submit this review via the API due to a permissions limitation on the provided token. The review content above is my complete assessment.*
